closed_won.size: Free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale; ~3,500 calls/month at 2 pilot locs (4 total; 14 physicians + 20 PAs, tripling via two acquisitions)
closed_won.champion: Jim (CEO); Deana Guy-Johnson (Clinical Manager, 10 yrs at GLOC)
closed_won.status: Signed April 27 — Solv's first Monarch design partner. Kennedy handling Maya setup (DID phone numbers, routing), Carli handling Athena 3rd-party API access and preview instance. Jim's leadership team on vacation until ~May 16; formal external kickoff call pending week of May 17. EPD strike team (Trevor, Kapeesh, Tucker, Carli) planning in-person collaboration in Northern Michigan.
key_risks: Phase 1 is $0 — margin begins only once resolution rate hits ~35%, unproven in ortho
Deana (Clinical Manager) openly skeptical AI can handle GLOC's 20+ appointment types per surgeon
Org just 3x'd in size via two acquisitions — internal chaos could slow implementation cooperation
closed_won.next_step: Kennedy to close Maya DID/phone routing requirements; Carli to close Athena 3rd-party auth. Create Monarch Drive folder + design partner onboarding SOP (per Kapeesh). Formal kickoff call post May 16. Owner: Brantley + implementation team.

active_deals.champion: Amanda (internal legal advocate); Todd Werner (President, requires final signature)
active_deals.status: 18+ months in discussion; now at a critical legal gate as of May 8. Todd Werner (Banner president) is requiring a 'term for convenience' clause as a condition of his fifth signature. Two paths on the table: restart the entire contract with the clause added, or sign today and amend immediately after. A direct meeting with Maria was scheduled May 8 to resolve this. Banner AVP texted the same day: 'Three years a long time in this day and age' but confirmed they're 'all in on making Solv work, want to expand beyond phase one.' Epic architectural review (2–3 months) still follows legal close.
key_risks: Todd Werner's 'term for convenience' demand risks restarting the full 90-day legal cycle
Epic architectural review adds 2–3 months after legal clears — implementation is still far out
18+ months in — Banner team fatigue and deal drift are real risks
active_deals.next_step: Resolve Werner's 'term for convenience' clause — sign now + amend, or restart. Owner: Teresa/Danny — outcome of May 8 Maria meeting unknown.
active_deals.leadership_table_note: Werner's convenience clause is the final gate; resolve via amendment or risk full contract restart

active_deals.size: $3,500/mo up to 3K telemed visits + $2K implementation + $0.50/ClearPay + 1% Solv Pay; 15–30 physicians in telehealth scope (250 total providers); ideal start date June 1
active_deals.champion: Abigail Escalante (ops/technical lead); Dr. Attia (clinical sponsor); Dr. Ammar Baba (decision-maker, engaged but skeptical)
active_deals.status: Full telehealth demo completed May 5. Dr. Baba engaged but guarded — has been burned by ECW integrations before, explicitly said 'if this doesn't integrate with ECW, let us know.' ECW integration proof (demo video + reference) is the explicit gate. Two deal tracks: UC Telemed (priority) and Specialties as a design partner target (Kapeesh call pending). CLS feedback compiled into a doc for Maria Kwok May 6. CLS pushing contract modifications: per-encounter or hybrid pricing, 3–6 months free/discounted ramp, 6-month initial term, waived $2K implementation fee. Daniele Farnedi taking over contract management. President engaged — sees potential to replace Luma for all primary care scheduling across 300+ providers.
key_risks: Dr. Baba burned by prior ECW integrations at scale — proof video and TX/multi-provider reference are the explicit gate items
HMO/PCP billing logic unresolved — place-of-service 20 workaround unconfirmed; many CLS patients have assigned PCPs
Pricing concession demands (flat fee → per-encounter, shorter term, waived implementation) all in flight simultaneously
active_deals.next_step: Deliver HIPAA-blurred ECW integration video + multi-provider ECW reference (Texas preferred) — Owner: Brantley/Danny — ASAP. Kapeesh to schedule specialties design partner scope call.
active_deals.leadership_table_note: ECW proof video + TX reference are the gate; Abby signaled mid-May close; contract mods in flight

Jon confirmed intent to return and said they are 'very interested.' No active engagement since April 7 — now 5+ weeks since last touch. Bryan joined the channel April 22 (role unknown).
key_risks: Assort exit timeline is entirely outside Solv's control — could be weeks or months
5+ weeks since last touch — Jon's internal advocacy may erode; check-in cadence has slipped
Competitor could step in during the open window; GLOC reference now available for when this resumes
active_deals.next_step: Check in with Jon on Assort exit status — overdue (was scheduled every 2 weeks). Clarify who Bryan is. Owner: Brantley — this week.

active_deals.size: 20 providers, 10-person contact center expanding to 16, $200K+ annual staffing cost; largest GI practice in New Mexico; 6-month waitlist
active_deals.ehr: Athena → Gastro by ModMed (gGastro), go-live June 16 (may slip)
active_deals.champion: Albert Lujan (Director, IT & Infrastructure) — controls vendor recommendations to board; shareholders pushing for AI scheduling
active_deals.status: Strong May 11 discovery call. Buyer intent is very high — every prior vendor has failed on gGastro integration, and Albert said 'if you can show a working proof of concept, it's almost a done deal.' Core pain: no weekend scheduling, phone tag, 50% revenue loss from unfilled cancellations. Critical blocker: gGastro has no confirmed write-back API — Kapeesh confirmed May 12 it is a distinct ModMed product requiring separate build decision. Jenn scoping ModMed synapSYS Marketplace (recommends $7,500 Elite tier for sandbox). Market sizing shows ~500 GI practices in ICP, 70–82% on gGastro. Albert is meeting with a Chicago GI group that may have a competitor with working ModMed AI scheduling.
key_risks: gGastro write-back API gap unconfirmed — every prior vendor hit this wall
Chicago competitor call could surface a working rival before Solv confirms feasibility
June 16 EMR go-live creates a tight window; missing it likely delays the deal months
active_deals.next_step: Confirm gGastro integration feasibility with Kapeesh/Jenn/Carli — follow up with Albert within 1–2 days (window from May 11 may already be passed). Owner: Brantley + Kapeesh.
active_deals.leadership_table_note: 'Almost a done deal' if gGastro integration is solvable; feasibility check overdue; Chicago competitor risk
